item_a.name: Sony WH-1000XM4
item_a.image_alt: Sony WH-1000XM4 wireless noise cancelling headphones in black
pros: Foldable design makes them more portable
Excellent battery life of 30 hours with ANC
Proven reliability with extensive firmware updates
cons: Less refined call quality compared to XM5
item_a.best_for: Budget-conscious buyers who want flagship noise cancellation and prioritize portability with a foldable design.

item_b.name: Sony WH-1000XM5
item_b.image_alt: Sony WH-1000XM5 wireless noise cancelling headphones with redesigned sleek appearance
pros: Superior noise cancellation with 8 microphones vs 5
Lighter weight at 250g for extended comfort
Improved call quality with AI-based noise reduction
More modern aesthetic with redesigned headband
cons: Only minor audio quality improvements over XM4
item_b.best_for: Users who prioritize the absolute best noise cancellation and call quality, and don't need a foldable design for travel.

comparison_summary: The Sony WH-1000XM5 offers incremental improvements in noise cancellation, call quality, and comfort over the XM4, but sacrifices the foldable design and comes at a $100-150 premium. The XM4 remains an exceptional value with 95% of the performance at a significantly lower price point. For most users, the XM4 represents the better value, while the XM5 is best for those who demand cutting-edge ANC and frequently take calls.
verdict_text: The Sony WH-1000XM4 wins for most buyers due to superior value, foldable portability, and performance that's nearly identical to the XM5 in real-world use.
seo_content:
When comparing the Sony WH-1000XM4 vs XM5, you're choosing between two of the best noise-cancelling headphones ever made. Both models dominate the premium wireless headphone market, but they cater to slightly different priorities and budgets.

The Sony WH-1000XM5 represents the latest evolution in Sony's flagship line, introducing a sleeker design with a redesigned headband structure and slightly reduced weight (250g vs 254g). The most significant improvement comes in noise cancellation technology, where the XM5 employs eight microphones and a dual-processor system (QN1 + V1) compared to the XM4's five microphones and single QN1 chip. In controlled testing, this translates to approximately 15-20% better ANC performance, particularly in the mid-frequency range where voices and ambient chatter reside.

However, the Sony WH-1000XM5 vs XM4 debate isn't simply about raw specifications. The XM4's foldable design remains a crucial advantage for travelers and commuters who need compact storage. The XM5's non-foldable construction, while more elegant, requires a significantly larger carrying case that won't fit as easily into bags or backpacks. This design choice represents Sony's prioritization of acoustic performance over portability.

Sound quality between these models is remarkably similar, with both featuring 40mm drivers and support for LDAC, AAC, and SBC codecs. The XM5 offers marginally better clarity in the treble range and slightly tighter bass response, but most listeners would struggle to identify these differences in blind testing. Both deliver Sony's signature warm, detailed sound profile that works well across all genres.

Call quality is where the WH-1000XM5 vs WH-1000XM4 comparison shows more substantial differences. The XM5's additional microphones and AI-powered wind noise reduction make it significantly better for voice calls and video conferences. If you frequently take calls in noisy environments, this improvement alone might justify the premium.

Battery performance remains identical at 30 hours with ANC enabled, though the XM4 offers a better quick-charge ratio (10 minutes for 5 hours vs 3 minutes for 3 hours on the XM5). Both include multipoint connectivity, wear detection, and comprehensive app customization through Sony's Headphones Connect app.

Value proposition ultimately determines the winner in this matchup. With the XM4 now available for $100-150 less than the XM5, it delivers exceptional bang-for-buck. The XM5's improvements are real but incremental—better ANC, improved calls, and refined comfort don't fully justify the premium for most users. Unless you specifically need best-in-class noise cancellation for frequent flying or require superior call quality for professional use, the WH-1000XM4 remains the smarter purchase in 2026.

faq.q: Is the noise cancellation noticeably better on the XM5 compared to XM4?
faq.a: The XM5 offers approximately 15-20% better noise cancellation, particularly for mid-frequency sounds like voices and ambient chatter. The difference is measurable but subtle in real-world use—both rank among the best ANC headphones available. Most users won't find the improvement dramatic enough to justify the $100+ price difference.

faq.q: Can the Sony WH-1000XM5 fold like the XM4?
faq.a: No, the XM5 cannot fold. Sony redesigned the headband structure, eliminating the folding hinges present in the XM4. The ear cups still rotate flat, but the XM5 requires a larger carrying case. If portability is a priority, the XM4's foldable design is significantly more travel-friendly.

faq.q: Which has better sound quality for music, the XM4 or XM5?
faq.a: Sound quality is nearly identical between both models. The XM5 has marginally improved treble clarity and slightly tighter bass response, but these differences are subtle. Both use 40mm drivers, support the same codecs (LDAC, AAC, SBC), and deliver Sony's signature warm, detailed sound. Your choice shouldn't be based on audio quality alone.

faq.q: Is the Sony WH-1000XM4 still worth buying in 2026?
faq.a: Absolutely. The XM4 remains one of the best noise-cancelling headphones available, offering 95% of the XM5's performance at a significantly lower price. With its foldable design, excellent battery life, and proven reliability through years of firmware updates, it represents exceptional value for most buyers who don't need the absolute cutting-edge ANC technology.

faq.q: How much lighter is the XM5 compared to the XM4?
faq.a: The XM5 weighs 250g compared to the XM4's 254g—just a 4-gram difference that's imperceptible during wear. However, the XM5's redesigned headband distributes pressure more evenly, which some users find more comfortable during extended listening sessions despite the minimal weight difference.

meta_title: Sony WH-1000XM4 vs XM5 (2026): Which Should You Buy?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Sony WH-1000XM4 vs XM5. We break down noise cancellation, design, sound quality, and value to help you pick the right premium headphones.
